body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Like the other rows of this cell, this is really an irrlicht-dispatcher test filed under the opencode adapter. The scenario co-launches an opencode process and at least one DIFFERENT-adapter process (claude/codex/pi/aider) in the same cwd and asserts that irrlicht reports distinct rows under their correct adapter labels with no cross-contamination.

The critical structural fact is that opencode's transport is disjoint from every partner's at the source-variant level:
1. **Process matching** — opencode declares its own ProcessMatcher (ExactName{Name: opencode}); the scanner runs each adapter's matcher independently and tags each PID with its originating adapter name.
2. **Session store** — opencode is the only ProcessOwnedStore adapter here: session state lives in a structured SQLite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) read by a MetricsReader that bypasses the JSONL-tailer path entirely, keyed on the session.directory column. The partner adapters live in FilesUnderRoot (claudecode ~/.claude/projects, codex ~/.codex/sessions, pi ~/.pi/agent/sessions) or FilesUnderCWD (aider .aider.chat.history.md) transports. There is NO shared reader, parser, or table that could leak state between opencode and a file-based adapter — opencode's store only ever contains opencode sessions.
3. **Per-session metrics** — SessionState carries an adapter-name field; metrics and parent linkage key off the (adapter, session_id) tuple. No global per-cwd aggregation in the domain.

Spec bullets map cleanly: each session appears with its correct adapter label (tagged at the matcher-driven scan / store read); states transition independently per agent (the opencode store poller and the partners' tailers are different goroutines reading different sources); no cross-contamination of metrics or linkage (keyed per (adapter, session_id)).

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

irrlicht has no first-class 'workspace' row — the dashboard groups by cwd visually but the daemon's session list is flat. That's correct here: the spec asks only about per-session correctness, not aggregate workspace state. opencode has no observable PID-to-transcript binding the way file-based adapters do; the spec is anchored on the working->ready ARC and session distinctness, not on a literal pid_discovered phase, so the ProcessOwnedStore transport satisfies it.

## Driver

The opencode interactive driver supports send/wait_turn/sleep — exactly the recipe shape this cell needs (a single short text turn). The driver originally hardcoded its own per-run cwd; for this cross-adapter cell it was extended (one line, mirroring the codex driver) to honor $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD so opencode shares the run-cell-multi.sh workspace with the partner agents. The SQLite session-lookup (directory = $RUN_CWD ORDER BY time_created DESC) still resolves opencode's own session unambiguously, since opencode.db never holds another adapter's rows.

## Recording

Recorded by run-cell-multi.sh in coexist mode, which launches every cross_adapter[] driver concurrently against ONE shared cwd under a single isolated --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each (this opencode cell stores the opencode transcript; its events.jsonl spans the whole workspace). opencode's default model is a slow local LM Studio model (~30-45s/turn), so its working span is long and it may enter working AFTER a faster partner — the adapter-agnostic spec (agentA = first to working, agentB = the other) validates either ordering.
caveats: opencode's default model (lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored, ~30-45s/turn) is slow; the spec's working-anchor windows are widened to 120s so a slow turn does not read as a missing transition. The recipe keeps opencode's send to a single short text turn so it finishes inside the multi-run window.

ProcessOwnedStore observability is harder to predict from docs than file-based transports; the LIVE recording is the authoritative check that opencode's session appears with its own working->ready arc distinct from the partner agents'.
Adapter labels are stable strings (opencode, claudecode, codex, pi, aider); the assertion is on the wire-level adapter name, not the macOS display string.

KNOWN FAILING (3/4): all 5 sessions distinguished (load-bearing passes), but agentB turn_done is a poll-race truncation when the headless process exits under concurrent load; daemon_capability=bug to match expected.jsonl known_failing. Tracked #492.

sources.note: opencode's ProcessOwnedStore reader polls the SQLite session table keyed by directory; it bypasses the JSONL tailer and never shares state with a file-based adapter's parser.
Parsers / PIDDiscoverers / ProcessNames / MetricsProviders are all keyed by adapter Identity.Name — opencode's MetricsReader is registered only for opencode sessions.

sources.ref: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace
sources.note: Three Expected bullets: correct adapter labels, independent transitions, no metrics/parent/subagent cross-contamination.
